content: The synchronous sequential circuit shown below works at a clock frequency of 1 GHz. The throughput, in Mbits/s, and the latency, in ns, respectively, are
explanation:
The given circuit is a Serial In Serial Out (SISO) shift register.

The clock frequency is provided as $1 \text{ GHz}$.

The clock period ($T_{\text{clk}}$) can be calculated as follows:

$ T_{\text{clk}} = \frac{1}{f_{\text{clk}}} = \frac{1}{10^9} \text{ seconds} = 10^{-9} \text{ seconds} = 1 \text{ ns} $

For a SISO shift register, the latency is determined by $N T_{\text{clk}}$, where $N$ represents the number of flip-flops.

$ \text{Latency} = N T_{\text{clk}} = 3 \times 1 \text{ ns} = 3 \text{ ns} $

The throughput is defined as the number of bits processed per second.

In this circuit, we obtain 1 bit of output every $T_{\text{clk}} = 1 \text{ ns}$.

Thus, the throughput is :

$ \text{Throughput} = 10^9 \text{ bits per second} = 10^3 \text{ Mbits per second} $

Therefore, the correct options are throughput of 1000 Mbits/s and latency of 3 ns.

content:
A traffic signal cycles from GREEN to YELLOW, YELLOW to RED and RED to GREEN.
In each cycle, GREEN is turned on for 70 seconds, YELLOW is turned on for
5 seconds and the RED is turned on for 75 seconds. This traffic light has to be implemented
using a finite state machine (FSM). The only input to this FSM is a clock of 5 second period.
The minimum number of flip-flops required to implement this FSM is _______.
explanation:
According to the question,

Green is turned ON for 70 seconds

Yellow is turned ON for 5 seconds.

Red is turned ON for 75 seconds

$$ \therefore $$ Total time to complete one cycle for all 3 lights = (70 + 5 +75) seconds = 150 seconds

Available time period of clock = 5 seconds

Total number of clock cycles in one complete cycle = $${{150} \over 5}$$ = 30

Assume number of flip-flops required = n

We know possible number of states using n flip-flops = $${2^n}$$

So to get 30 clock cycle or 30 states,

$${2^n}$$ $$ \ge $$ 30

$$ \Rightarrow $$ n $$ \ge $$ 4.90

$$ \therefore $$ Minimum number of required flip-flops is = 5

content:
In the circuit shown below, the AND gate has a propagation delay of 1 ns . The edgetriggered flip-flops have a set-up time of 2 ns , a hold-time of 0 ns , and a clock-to-Q delay of 2 ns .

The maximum clock frequency (in MHz , rounded off to the nearest integer) such that there are no setup violations is___________ .
explanation:
In the given circuit, we need to determine the maximum clock frequency to avoid setup violations.

Parameters:

Propagation delay of AND gate: 1 ns

Setup time of flip-flops: 2 ns

Hold time of flip-flops: 0 ns

Clock-to-Q delay of flip-flops: 2 ns

Calculation:

Total clock-to-data path delay for Flip-Flop 1 ($ T_{\text{clkD1}} $):

$ T_{\text{clkD1}} = t_{\text{p}} + t_{\text{s}} + t_{\text{comb}} $

Plug in the values:

$ T_{\text{clkD1}} = 2\, \text{ns} + 2\, \text{ns} + 1\, \text{ns} = 5\, \text{ns} $

Clock-to-data path delay for Flip-Flop 2 ($ T_{\text{clkD2}} $):

$ T_{\text{clkD2}} = t_{\text{s}} + t_{\text{p}} $

Plug in the values:

$ T_{\text{clkD2}} = 2\, \text{ns} + 2\, \text{ns} = 4\, \text{ns} $

Determine the Required Clock Period:

The clock period must be the maximum of the calculated delays to ensure there are no setup violations:

$ T_{\text{clk}} = \max(T_{\text{clkD1}}, T_{\text{clkD2}}) = \max(5\, \text{ns}, 4\, \text{ns}) = 5\, \text{ns} $

Calculate Maximum Clock Frequency:

Finally, the maximum clock frequency is given by the reciprocal of the clock period:

$ f_{\text{max}} = \frac{1}{T_{\text{clk}}} = \frac{1}{5\, \text{ns}} = 200\, \text{MHz} $

Thus, the maximum clock frequency is 200 MHz.

content: The sequence of states $(Q_1 Q_0)$ of the given synchronous sequential circuit is ________.
explanation:
$$ \text { Given : Sequential Circuit is shown below } $$

Input to each $T$ flip flop is given below : $T_1=Q_1+\bar{Q}_0$ and $T_0=Q_0+Q_1$

State table of the give sequential circuit is,

$$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|} \hline \text { Clock } & T_1=Q_1+\bar{Q}_0 & T_0=Q_0+Q_1 & Q_1^{+} & Q_0^{+} \\ \hline \text { Initially } & - & - & 0 & 0 \\ \hline \uparrow & 1 & 0 & 1 & 0 \\ \hline \uparrow & 1 & 1 & 0 & 1 \\ \hline \uparrow & 0 & 1 & 0 & 0 \\ \hline \end{array} $$

So, sequence of state of given sequential circuit is, $00 \rightarrow 10 \rightarrow 01 \rightarrow 00$. Hence, the correct option is (B).

content: In a given sequential circuit, initial states are Q$$_1$$ = 1 and Q$$_2$$ = 0. For a clock frequency of 1 MHz, the frequency of signal Q$$_2$$ in kHz, is ___________ (rounded off to the nearest integer).
explanation:
Given circuit is

$$ D_1=Q_2, D_2=\bar{Q}_1 $$

Initially $Q_1=1, Q_2=0$

$$ \begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|} \hline \text { CLK } & \boldsymbol{D}_{\mathbf{1}}=\boldsymbol{Q}_{\mathbf{2}} & \boldsymbol{D}_{\mathbf{2}}=\overline{\boldsymbol{Q}}_1 & \boldsymbol{Q}_1 & \boldsymbol{Q}_2 & \\ \hline \text { Initially } & & & 1 & 0 & \\ \hline 1 & 0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & \\ \hline 2 & 0 & 1 & 0 & 1 & \\ \hline 3 & 1 & 1 & 1 & 1 & \\ \hline 4 & 1 & 0 & 1 & 0 \rightarrow & \text { repeat } \\ \hline \end{array} $$

Hence, given counter having MOD-4 output frequency,

$$ f_0=\frac{f_i}{4}=\frac{1000 k}{4}=250 \mathrm{kHz} $$

Hence, the correct answer is 250 .

content: A state transition diagram with states A, B, and C, and transition probabilities p1, p2, ....., p7 is shown in the figure (e.g., p1 denotes the probability of transition from state A to B). For this state diagram, select the statements which is/are universally true.

The propagation delay of the exclusive-OR (XOR) gate in the circuit in the figure is 3 ns . The propagation delay of all the flip-flops is assumed to be Zero. The clock (Clk) frequency provided to the circuit is 500 MHz .

Starting from the initial value of the flip-flop outputs $Q_2 Q_1 Q_0=111$ with $D_2=1$, the minimum number of triggering clock edges after which the flip-flop outputs $Q_2 Q_1 Q_0$ becomes 100 (in integer) is $\_\_\_\_$
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { As mentioned in the question, we have }\\ &\begin{aligned} & f_{\text {clock }}=500 \mathrm{MHZ} \\ & \text { } \begin{aligned} T & =\frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{100} \times 10^{-6} \\ Also,& =0.2 \times 10^{-8} \\ & =2 \mathrm{~ms} \end{aligned} \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

Now, we get the minimum number of triggering clock edges after the output of flip flop, $Q_2 Q_1 Q_0$ become $100=4$. Hence, the correct answer is $(4$ to 4$)$.

content: For the components in the sequential circuit shown below, $t_{p d}$ is the propagation delay, $t_{\text {secup }}$ is the setup time and $t_{\text {hold }}$ is the hold time. The maximum clock frequency (rounded off to the nearest integer), at which the given circuit can operate reliably, is $\_\_\_\_$ MHz.
explanation:
$$ \text { Given circuit is shown below, } $$

Setup time $\left(t_{s u}\right)$ is the time required to set the input at proper level before the application of clock active edge. Hold time $\left(t_h\right)$ is the minimum time required to maintain input at its level after the application of clock for proper output

Given for Flip flop-1,

$$ \begin{aligned} & t_{p d_1}=3 \mathrm{~ns} \\ & t_{s u_1}=5 \mathrm{~ns} \\ & t_{h_1}=1 \mathrm{~ns} \end{aligned} $$

For flip flop-2,

$$ \begin{aligned} & t_{p d_2}=8 \mathrm{~ns} \\ & t_{s u_2}=4 \mathrm{~ns} \end{aligned} $$

$$ t_{h_2}=3 \mathrm{~ns} $$

The operations that are required to perform in between two clock active edges decides the clock frequency for proper operation. It can be assumed to be divided between two stages for cross coupled flip flops.

The operations required are as follows :

If we start from flip flop-2 after $t_{p d_2}=8 \mathrm{~ns}$ of active edge of clock, output of flip flop-2 gets settled this output is feeded as input to flip flop- 1 , So it must be stable for at least $t_{s u_1}=5 \mathrm{~ns}$.

So the total time required during this process,

$$ T_1=t_{p d_2}+t_{s u_1}=8+5=13 \mathrm{~ns} $$

Now starting from flip flop 1 ,

After $t_{p d_1}=3 \mathrm{~ns}$ of application of clock active edge, the output of flip flop- 1 will get settled. So after 3 ns , the input is ready to be applied at the EXOR gate which has a propagation delay of 2 ns .

Hence after $3+2=5 \mathrm{~ns}$, the input is ready to be applied at the NAND gate which has $t_{p d}=2 \mathrm{~ns}$.

So finally after $5+2=7 \mathrm{~ns}$ of clock, input of flip flop- 2 is ready but it must be stable for $t_{s u_2}=4 \mathrm{~ns}$. The total time required during this process.

$$ \begin{aligned} & T_2=t_{p d_1}+\left(t_{p d}\right)_{E X O R}+\left(t_{p d}\right)_{N A N D}+t_{s u_2} \\ & T_2=3+2+2+4=11 \mathrm{nsec} \end{aligned} $$

For proper operation minimum difference time between active edges of clock, i.e.

$$ \begin{aligned} & \left(T_{c l k}\right)_{\min }=\text { Maximum of }\left(T_1 \text { and } T_2\right)=13 \mathrm{nsec} \\ & \left(f_{c l k}\right)_{\max }=\frac{1}{13 \mathrm{~ns}}=\frac{1000}{13} \mathrm{MHz}=76.92 \mathrm{MHz} \end{aligned} $$

Nearest integer $=77$

Hence, the maximum clock frequency (rounded off to the nearest integer), at which the given circuit can operate reliably, is $\mathbf{7 7 ~ M H z}$.

For M=1, sequence of Q1, Q0 is ..00, 01, 10, 11, 00, 01.......
For M=0, sequence of Q1, Q0 is ..00, 11, 10, 01, 00, 11......

(a)Design the necessary feedback logic for T1 and T0.

(b)Realize the feesback logic using inverters and 4-input multiplexers only. Use Q1 and Q0 as the control inputs of the multiplexer with Q1 as the MSB.
